source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AB \parallel CD$, $\angle FGB = 155^\circ$, $FG$ bisects $\angle EFD$, what is the size of $\angle BEF$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $AB\parallel CD$ and $\angle FGB=155^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle BEF+\angle EFD=180^\circ$,\\
hence $\angle GFD=180^\circ-\angle FGB=180^\circ-155^\circ=25^\circ$,\\
Given that $FG$ bisects $\angle EFD$,\\
it implies $\angle EFD=2\angle GFD=2\times 25^\circ=50^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle BEF=180^\circ-\angle EFD=180^\circ-50^\circ=\boxed{130^\circ}$.

multi_step: The provided solution uses multiple linked inferences: derive ∠GFD from ∠FGB using parallel-line angle relations, use the angle bisector condition to get ∠EFD, then use a supplementary-angle relation to obtain ∠BEF
This is not a direct lookup; it combines at least three reasoning steps
The problem is geometric and diagram-based, which supports chained visual-text reasoning
verifiability: The target answer is a single numeric angle measure (130°), which is easy to check
The provided answer_text gives an explicit step-by-step derivation that can be validated rule-by-rule
registry_passed=true, and both question and answer are present in the registry record
